## Priors

The priors below are documented in `priors.py` (the single source of truth, so
they cannot drift between models). The **role** column separates the *causal*
prior (the randomised effect) from *precision* terms (which sharpen it but do not
license the causal claim), *association* couplings (adjusted, never "drives"),
*nuisance* terms (intercept / dispersion / random-intercept scale), and any *GP*
amplitudes / lengthscales.

```{python}
# | echo: false
priors_table if priors_table is not None else "No priors table available."
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Panels for the priors this model actually registered (the dead panels are
# pruned at fit time; #125 Area 1).
_seen = set()
if priors_table is not None and "panel" in priors_table.columns:
    for _pan in priors_table["panel"].dropna():
        if _pan and _pan not in _seen and _has(f"prior_{_pan}.png"):
            _seen.add(_pan)
            print(f"![{_pan}](prior_{_pan}.png){{width=32%}}")
```

### Prior pushforward to the estimand scale

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# What the prior on the effect implies on the items scale *before* seeing data
# (#125 Area 1/2 estimand check).
_pf = _csv("prior_pushforward.csv")
if _pf is not None:
    _r = _pf.iloc[0]
    print(
        f"Before seeing data, the prior on the treatment effect implies an "
        f"items-scale average marginal effect with median "
        f"**{_r.prior_items_median:+.1f}**, a 50% range "
        f"{_r.prior_items_lo50:+.1f} to {_r.prior_items_hi50:+.1f}, and a 95% range "
        f"{_r.prior_items_lo:+.1f} to {_r.prior_items_hi:+.1f} "
        f"(denominator {int(_r.n_trials)}). A well-calibrated prior is wide but not "
        f"absurd — it should not concentrate mass on implausibly large effects."
    )
else:
    print("_No prior pushforward available for this model._")
```

### Prior vs posterior

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("prior_posterior.png", "Prior vs posterior overlay"))
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Power-scaling prior/likelihood sensitivity (psense)"}

Sensitivity of each parameter to power-scaling its prior and likelihood
(Kallioinen et al. 2024); a `✓` flags no concern. Recommended-but-secondary at
this sample size.

```{python}
# | echo: false
_ps = _csv("psense_summary.csv", index_col=0)
_ps if _ps is not None else "No psense summary."
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("psense.png", "Power-scaling sensitivity"))
```

:::
